name: OPEC+
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **WORLD BANK $86 AVERAGE FOR 2026 + GOLDMAN Q2 $90 FORECAST = INSTITUTIONAL ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF MOU PEACE-DIVIDEND PRICING.** Brent collapsed -7% May 6 to ~$103 on Axios MOU scoop; trading $101.95 May 7 morning — the institutional forecast band of $86-90 for 2026 average is now in-range. Saudi Tadawul -0.5% only major equity index in red on global risk-on day reflects peace-dividend pricing bearish for the war's primary fiscal beneficiary. Saudi MBS Day 9 of 14 silence on UAE OPEC exit (effective May 1) and May 4 Iran-Fujairah strike continues — UAE foreign minister rather than ruler at Tuesday Jeddah summit deepens diplomatic distance. June 7 OPEC+ ministerial remains the structural test for substantive Saudi response; with UAE OUT, the post-OPEC architecture rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait alone. Atlantic Council framing: UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity medium-term-BEARISH (12-24mo) for Brent. WoodMac: ADNOC Murban discount potentially widening to $3.50/bbl by Q3. **Critical contradiction in MOU framework:** if Iran ships out HEU and US lifts sanctions per the MOU provisions, eventual Iranian crude return to global market adds another medium-term bearish factor on top of UAE OPEC exit and World Bank/Goldman forecast cuts.

current.saudi_position: carrying more burden post-UAE; MBS publicly silent; needs higher revenue post-Sadara damage
